steps.explanation: Apply the Factor Theorem: if $f(a) = 0$, then $(x - a)$ is a factor of $f(x)$. The three x-intercepts give three zeros: $x = -3$, $x = 1$, and $x = 4$.
Identify which factor appears among the answer choices. The factors are $(x+3)$, $(x-1)$, and $(x-4)$. Only $(x-1)$ matches a listed choice.

Evaluate all choices:
  • Choice A: $x+1$ corresponds to zero $x = -1$, which is not an x-intercept. Not necessarily a factor.
  • Choice B: $x+4$ corresponds to zero $x = -4$, which is not an x-intercept. Not necessarily a factor.
  • Choice C: $x-1$ corresponds to zero $x = 1$, which is an x-intercept. This must be a factor. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • Choice D: $x-3$ corresponds to zero $x = 3$, which is not an x-intercept. Not necessarily a factor.
